panels.title: Query Throughput
panels.description: Number of chat requests per hour. Shows how many questions users ask the RAG system over time.

targets.rawSql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('hour', created_at) AS time, COUNT(*) FROM conversations GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Retrieval Latency (ms)
panels.description: Average time spent searching Qdrant (hybrid dense+sparse + cross-encoder reranker). Lower is better — high values may indicate Qdrant overload.

targets.rawSql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('hour', created_at) AS time, AVG(retrieval_latency_ms)::int FROM conversations GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: LLM Latency (ms)
panels.description: Average time GPT-4o takes to generate an answer. High values may indicate complex questions or OpenAI API slowdowns.

targets.rawSql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('hour', created_at) AS time, AVG(llm_latency_ms)::int FROM conversations GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Token Usage
panels.description: Prompt and completion tokens sent to GPT-4o per hour. Useful for tracking OpenAI API costs.

targets.rawSql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('hour', created_at) AS time, SUM(prompt_tokens) AS prompt, SUM(completion_tokens) AS completion FROM conversations GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Error Rate (%)
panels.description: Percentage of chat requests that failed with an exception (e.g. Qdrant down, OpenAI API errors, embedding failures). 0% means all requests completed successfully.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE error IS NOT NULL AND error != '') * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1) AS pct FROM conversations

panels.title: Total Queries
panels.description: Total number of chat questions asked since the system started.

panels.title: Feedback Score (avg)
panels.description: Average user feedback score (1-5). Only considers conversations where feedback was submitted.

targets.rawSql: SELECT ROUND(COALESCE(AVG(feedback), 0), 2) FROM conversations WHERE feedback != 0

panels.title: Total Response Time (ms)
panels.description: End-to-end latency from receiving the question to returning the answer (embedding + retrieval + LLM). The most important metric for user experience.

targets.rawSql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('hour', created_at) AS time, AVG(total_latency_ms)::int FROM conversations GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
